skill.name: azure-web-pubsub-ts
skill.description: Build real-time messaging applications using Azure Web PubSub SDKs for JavaScript (@azure/web-pubsub, @azure/web-pubsub-client). Implement WebSocket-based pub/sub messaging, group management, and connection handling.
skill.summary: Real-time WebSocket messaging with Azure Web PubSub SDK for TypeScript and JavaScript applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains legitimate Azure Web PubSub SDK documentation. Static analyzer produced 35 false positive external_commands findings by misinterpreting markdown code block backticks as shell execution. Network and environment variable patterns are standard Azure SDK usage. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detection
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 35 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code block delimiters in documentation, not actual shell command execution. No child_process or exec usage present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: File contains only TypeScript code examples in markdown format. Backticks are documentation syntax, not code execution.

low_findings.title: Standard Network Usage
low_findings.description: fetch() call at line 155 is legitimate WebSocket negotiation pattern for Azure Web PubSub client connection. URL is relative endpoint '/negotiate', not external hardcoded URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Azure Web PubSub client negotiation flow documented in official SDK examples.

low_findings.title: Standard Environment Variable Configuration
low_findings.description: Environment variable access for WEBPUBSUB_CONNECTION_STRING and WEBPUBSUB_ENDPOINT follows Azure SDK best practices. These are configuration patterns, not credential leakage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Azure SDK requires endpoint configuration via environment variables. No sensitive data exposure or exfiltration patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build Real-Time Apps with Azure Web PubSub
content.value_statement: Implement WebSocket-based real-time messaging in your applications using Azure Web PubSub SDKs. This skill provides production-ready patterns for pub/sub messaging, group management, and connection handling with TypeScript.
actual_capabilities: Generate client access tokens with user authentication and custom roles
Send broadcast, user-targeted, and group-specific messages
Manage connection groups and handle WebSocket events
Implement Express middleware for real-time event handlers
Authenticate clients using Azure Entra ID or access keys
Handle connection lifecycle events (connected, disconnected, rejoin)
limitations: Requires Azure Web PubSub resource and valid connection string or endpoint
Client-side code requires server-side negotiate endpoint implementation
Express handler requires Express.js framework integration
Does not cover advanced scaling or multi-region deployment patterns

use_cases.title: Real-Time Chat Application
use_cases.description: Build a multi-room chat system with user authentication, group messaging, and presence indicators using Azure Web PubSub groups and user-targeted messaging.

use_cases.title: Live Dashboard Updates
use_cases.description: Push real-time data updates to connected dashboards using broadcast messaging. Ideal for monitoring systems, analytics displays, or live metrics visualization.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers implementing real-time data streaming

use_cases.title: Multiplayer Game Lobby
use_cases.description: Create game rooms with player matchmaking, in-game messaging, and session management using groups and connection management APIs.
use_cases.target_user: Game developers adding real-time multiplayer features

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Azure Web PubSub server-side client using the connection string from environment variables. Create a hub named 'chat' and generate a basic client access token.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure Web PubSub integration

prompt_templates.title: Authenticated Token Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a client access token for user 'user123' with roles for joining groups, sending to groups, and auto-join the 'chat-room' group. Set expiration to 60 minutes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing user authentication with custom permissions

prompt_templates.title: Client-Side Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WebPubSubClient that connects via a negotiate endpoint. Register handlers for connected, group-message, and disconnected events. Join the 'chat-room' group after connection.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a real-time client with event handling

prompt_templates.title: Express Event Handler
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement an Express middleware handler for the 'chat' hub with handleConnect validation, handleUserEvent processing, and onConnected logging. Include a negotiate endpoint that generates tokens for authenticated users.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production-ready server with authentication and event handling

output_examples.input: Generate client token with user ID and group membership
output_examples.output: Token generated with URL wss://resource.webpubsub.azure.com/client/access/hubs/chat?access_token=xxx, userId=user123, groups=[chat-room], roles=[webpubsub.joinLeaveGroup, webpubsub.sendToGroup]

output_examples.input: Send message to all users in chat-room group
output_examples.output: Message broadcast successfully to 12 connections in group chat-room. Message content: {type: 'message', data: 'Hello everyone!'}

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production instead of access keys for better security
Register all event handlers before calling client.start() to avoid missing initial events
Validate authentication in handleConnect to reject unauthorized connections early in the handshake
anti_patterns: Hardcoding connection strings or access keys directly in source code instead of using environment variables
Accessing process.env variables without TypeScript non-null assertion or fallback values
Sending messages before verifying client connection state is 'connected'

faq.question: What is the difference between @azure/web-pubsub and @azure/web-pubsub-client?
faq.answer: @azure/web-pubsub is the server-side SDK for managing hubs, generating tokens, and sending messages. @azure/web-pubsub-client is the browser/Node.js client SDK for connecting to WebSocket endpoints and handling real-time messages.

faq.question: How do I authenticate users with Azure Web PubSub?
faq.answer: Use either Azure Entra ID with DefaultAzureCredential (recommended for production) or generate tokens with userId and roles using the server SDK. Clients connect using the generated token URL.

faq.question: What happens when a client disconnects unexpectedly?
faq.answer: Azure Web PubSub automatically attempts reconnection. Handle the 'disconnected' event for cleanup and 'rejoin-group-failed' to handle cases where group rejoin fails after reconnection.

faq.question: Can I send messages to specific users versus all users?
faq.answer: Yes. Use sendToAll() for broadcast, sendToUser() for user-targeted messages (delivers to all their connections), or sendToConnection() for a specific connection ID.

faq.question: How do groups work in Azure Web PubSub?
faq.answer: Groups are logical channels within a hub. Users can join/leave groups dynamically. Messages sent to a group reach all connections in that group. Useful for chat rooms, game lobbies, or topic-based subscriptions.

faq.question: What is the negotiate endpoint pattern?
faq.answer: The negotiate endpoint is a server route that generates client access tokens. Clients call this endpoint to get a WebSocket URL with access token, then connect using that URL. This keeps credentials server-side.
